Name: Brown Space Engineering (BSE)
Relevance: Designs, builds, and launches CubeSats; notable for the EQUiSat mission launched in 2018.

Name: Brown Rocketry Team
Relevance: Active student group focused on high-powered rocketry competitions and propulsion development.

Name: NASA Rhode Island Space Grant Consortium
Relevance: Headquartered at Brown; supports space research, fellowships, and aerospace-related outreach across RI.

Name: Department of Earth, Environmental and Planetary Sciences (DEEPS)
Relevance: Core academic hub for planetary geology, atmospheric science, and exploration of solar system bodies.

Name: Brown University Astronomy and Astrophysics Group
Relevance: Conducts research in observational cosmology, galaxy evolution, and high-energy astrophysics.

Name: Planetary Geosciences Group
Relevance: Focused on the surface processes and evolution of planets, including Mars and Moon exploration data analysis.

Name: Humans To Robots Laboratory (h2r)
Relevance: Researches robot teleoperation and human-robot interaction, applicable to remote planetary exploration.

Name: Intelligent Robot Lab (IRL)
Relevance: Develops autonomous systems and planning algorithms relevant to autonomous space probes and rovers.

Name: Ladd Observatory
Relevance: Historic university observatory used for astronomical observation, research, and public science outreach.

Name: Brown University Robotics Club
Relevance: General student robotics group that often explores autonomous systems and competitive design.

Name: Atmospheric Science Group
Relevance: Studies planetary atmospheres and climate dynamics, extending to solar system atmospheric models.

Name: Brown Institute for Molecular and Nanoscale Innovation (IMNI)
Relevance: Research into nanomaterials often used in aerospace composites and radiation-hardened electronics.

Name: Planetary Data Center (PDC)
Relevance: Maintains archives of planetary images and mission data, supporting planetary research across the university.

Name: Brown High Altitude Ballooning (BHAB)
Relevance: Former/occasional student initiative for near-space exploration and payload testing via high-altitude balloons.

Name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA) - Brown Chapter
Relevance: Student chapter of the professional aerospace society; facilitates networking and technical projects.

Name: Brown Design Workshop (BDW)
Relevance: Maker space where many space-related student projects (BSE, Rocketry) fabricate components.

Name: Plasma Physics Research Group
Relevance: Studies fundamental plasma properties, which are critical for electric propulsion and space weather understanding.
